INSTITUTIONAL FOLLOW-THROUGH STUDY

Day 2: The Follow-Through

A study of the “Day 2” continuation setup — buying or shorting a stock the morning after a big, high-volume, conviction-close move. Part 1 shows the daily pattern across 841 setups (May 2025–May 2026); Part 2 stress-tests it on realized intraday data; Part 3 is the one-page verdict. Read top to bottom.

The whole study on one plate. The six daily findings at a glance — full detail and the intraday reality check follow below.

PART 1   The Daily Setup — A Strong-Looking Pattern

Each setup is a fully-qualified Day-1 footprint. The daily evidence below shows the continuation pattern exists; whether it is tradable comes in Part 2.

PART 2   The Intraday Reality Check — Is It Tradable?

Realized Polygon 1-minute data on a 51-setup pilot. This is where the headline number meets reality.

PART 3   Findings — One Page
Data: 841 Day 2 setups across 666 tickers, May 2025 – May 2026 · intraday pilot n = 51, Polygon 1-minute bars. Continuation = the Day-2 open gapped in the setup’s direction. R = risk to the stop below the turn. All figures are embedded in this file — select all, copy, and paste into a document to keep the images.
